app.name: 360 Render
app.description: Private 360 panorama render studio for architects and interior designers.
app.currentMvp: A Next.js app with a public marketing homepage, Supabase email/password auth protecting Supabase-backed project and scene data, a project gallery, a real beta upload-to-render workspace flow, an immersive 360 panorama viewer, and server-side render pipeline endpoints for OpenAI image edits, Topaz upscaling, Supabase storage, and daily render limits.

techStack.auth: Supabase Auth through @supabase/ssr cookie-based server-side clients and Next.js proxy route protection
techStack.animation: GSAP for public homepage ScrollTrigger effects and reduced-motion-aware auth form mode transitions
techStack.storage: Supabase Storage through a server-only admin client
techStack.database: Supabase Postgres schema in docs/supabase-schema.sql and supabase/migrations/20260531000000_functional_hardening.sql
techStack.imageRendering: OpenAI Images API via server-only helper
techStack.upscaling: Topaz Labs Image API via server-only helper

appShell.responsibility: Defines app metadata, imports global CSS, and renders the root HTML/body wrapper.

productDesignContext.responsibility: Strategic design context for the public brand surface: register, audience, product purpose, brand personality, anti-references, principles, and accessibility notes.

publicRoot.responsibility: Public homepage with minimal dark product positioning over a full-screen interactive panorama hero, hero CTA, workflow steps, feature cards, showcase, pricing, footer CTAs, anchor links, and placeholder action buttons.

homeInteractions.responsibility: Client-side wrapper for homepage-only placeholder actions, newsletter submit prevention, and toast-style click feedback without navigating away.

homeScrollEffects.responsibility: Homepage-only client component that registers GSAP ScrollTrigger in the browser, runs reduced-motion-aware scroll reveal and subtle hero parallax animations, refreshes after load, and cleans up scoped triggers on unmount.

loginPage.responsibility: Server-rendered login route that resolves safe next-path and status/error messages, then renders the client login form with Supabase Auth server actions.

loginForm.responsibility: Client-side visual auth form over the generated architectural background, including a GSAP-animated sign in/create account mode switch, email/password fields, confirm-password signup field, password reveal, Supabase submits, and password recovery submit.

loginActions.responsibility: Server actions for signInWithPassword, signUp, and resetPasswordForEmail, including safe next-path redirects, confirm-password signup validation, BETA_ALLOWED_EMAILS allowlist gating capped at 5 emails, and signup/recovery email redirect metadata.

authConfirmRoute.responsibility: Handles Supabase email confirmation token_hash callbacks and redirects confirmed users into the protected workspace.

workspaceRoutes.responsibility: Protected App Router pages that load Supabase-backed workspace data and mount WorkspaceApp on the Projects or Viewer screen.

workspaceClientExperience.responsibility: Client-side project gallery, create-project panel, upload/render panel, and panorama viewer UI. Receives Supabase-backed project and scene data, creates projects through /api/projects, submits real render jobs through /api/render-jobs, polls Topaz status until ready/failed, updates scenes with signed image URLs, maintains local navigation state, and exposes logout.

heroPanoramaPreview.responsibility: Client-side full-screen interactive panorama layer for the public homepage hero, mounting PanoramaSphere with the generated 2:1 penthouse panorama, drag-to-rotate interaction, slow idle autorotation, and mousewheel zoom disabled for page scrolling.

authProxy.responsibility: Refreshes Supabase SSR auth cookies and redirects guests away from /projects and /viewer while redirecting logged-in users away from /login.

supabaseSsrClients.responsibility: Browser and per-request server Supabase clients for cookie-based Auth sessions. Server client falls back to existing non-public env names for local compatibility.

globalStyles.responsibility: Dark desktop-style app chrome, minimal full-bleed public homepage layout with borderless glass accents and motion, placeholder action toast styling, project grid/list layouts, viewer overlays, login form styling, responsive behavior, and entrance animations.

panoramaViewer.responsibility: Renders a hydration-stable empty viewer shell, detects WebGL support after mount, dynamically imports Photo Sphere Viewer on the client, creates/destroys the viewer instance, supports configurable mousewheel zoom and opt-in reduced-motion-aware idle autorotation, exposes a minimal controller, and falls back to a static image when WebGL is unavailable.

renderJobsApi.responsibility: Authenticated multipart render-job endpoint. Production defaults to real mode; local development defaults to mock unless RENDER_PROVIDER_MODE overrides it. Real mode enforces a 3-per-user daily real render limit, uploads the source image, renders through OpenAI, stores the OpenAI output, starts a Topaz upscale, records provider metadata, returns an interim signed URL, and marks failed provider/storage work.

projectsApi.responsibility: Authenticated JSON endpoint for creating owner-scoped Supabase project rows.

topazStatusApi.responsibility: Authenticated Topaz status polling endpoint that verifies the process belongs to the current user's render job, returns download metadata when complete, uploads the final Topaz asset to Supabase Storage, returns a signed final URL, and marks the scene/job ready or failed.

workspaceData.responsibility: Server-only Supabase workspace loader that maps projects, scenes, and latest render jobs into WorkspaceApp view models with empty/error states and signed URLs for private stored panorama assets.

authHelpers.responsibility: Server-only helper for authenticated route handlers, using Supabase getClaims and shared 401 JSON responses.

supabaseServer.responsibility: Server-only Supabase admin client singleton using SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY.

panoramaStorage.responsibility: Uploads panorama assets to the configured Supabase Storage bucket and creates signed URLs for stored assets.

renderLimits.responsibility: Defines beta render provider mode defaults, the 3-per-user daily real render limit, UTC daily windows, and render capacity checks.

betaAuthAllowlist.responsibility: Normalizes and parses BETA_ALLOWED_EMAILS, caps configured beta testers to 5 emails, checks app-level beta auth access, and validates signup password confirmation.

domainLibraries.responsibility: Render status types, panorama validation, render presets, and richer mock data that is still only partially wired into the visible workspace.

supabaseSchema.responsibility: Planned persistence model for projects, scenes, render jobs, private storage bucket, and owner-based row level security policies.

frontendArchitecture.renderingModel: Protected workspace routes load Supabase-backed workspace data on the server, then hydrate a client component for local state, browser URL APIs, file upload validation, render submission, polling, and imperative viewer controls. Login and route protection are server-rendered/server-action driven.

routes.behavior: Public homepage with Start Free and pricing CTAs linking to /login, workflow/showcase navigation, and View All Projects linking to protected /projects.

Reference-aligned full-screen auth page with a same-panel GSAP transition between sign in and create account modes, app-level beta email allowlist gating, password reveal, confirm-password signup validation, and Supabase password recovery. Logged-in users are redirected to /projects by proxy.
Protected 360 panorama viewer opened from a project card or direct query param.

screens.name: Homepage
features: brand navigation with Pricing, Showcase, and Log in links

minimal full-screen dark hero with the interactive 360 panorama as the background, floating navigation, copy, CTA, slow idle autorotation, drag-to-rotate interaction, and no visible hero viewer chrome
4-step workflow section for upload, preset selection, render/upscale, and viewer sharing with extra top breathing room after the hero
Render, Upscale, and Present feature cards with panorama imagery
showcase project cards using local panorama assets and reference-aligned project labels
static pricing section with Free, Pro, and Studio plans, Yearly selected, and Save up to 30% billing UI
placeholder footer, social, billing, legal, and newsletter actions with in-page feedback

monochrome dark gray and off-black CSS treatment with borderless glass accents, soft light beams, panorama imagery, transform/opacity entrance motion, GSAP ScrollTrigger scroll reveals and subtle hero parallax, hover/press motion, and reduced-motion handling

screens.name: Login
features: confirm password input in create account mode
log in action through existing Supabase server action
same-panel create account mode through existing Supabase signup server action
app-level beta tester email allowlist limited to the first 5 configured emails
GSAP opacity/translate form transition with reduced-motion handling

screens.name: Projects
features: top bar with Projects and Viewer tabs
preset selection and real render job submit
project cards with persisted scene counts and status badges

navigation.type: Local React state with route/query-string persistence

assets.panoramaImages: public/panoramas/*.jpg plus public/panoramas/generated-hero-panorama.png for the homepage viewer preview, Render feature card, and Penthouse Suite showcase card
assets.authLoginBackground: public/auth-login-background.png generated for the reference-style login screen

apiRoutes.input: Supabase token_hash, type, and optional next query params
apiRoutes.behavior: Verifies the OTP token through Supabase Auth and redirects to the protected workspace or login error state.
Requires Supabase Auth, creates an owner-scoped project row, and returns the created project.

apiRoutes.input: multipart/form-data with image File, presetId string, optional notes string, optional projectId string, optional sceneName string, and optional mode string
apiRoutes.auth: Requires Supabase Auth and returns 401 for guests.
apiRoutes.mockBehavior: Mock mode is retained for local development and creates Supabase scene/render_job records with status rendering_openai while skipping provider calls.
realBehavior: enforce the daily real render limit
upload source asset to Supabase Storage
create Supabase scene and render_job records scoped to the current user's project
call OpenAI image edits with the selected render preset
upload OpenAI render output to Supabase Storage
return a signed OpenAI interim image URL
return job, storage, OpenAI, Topaz metadata, and remaining daily quota
mark scene/job failed with a readable message on provider or storage failure

apiRoutes.input: Topaz process id in the route segment
apiRoutes.auth: Requires Supabase Auth and returns 401 for guests.
apiRoutes.behavior: Fetches Topaz status for an owned render job, adds download metadata when completed, stores the final Topaz output, returns a signed final URL, and marks records ready or failed.

providerFlow: Authenticated user submits a 2:1 panorama image and render preset.
Server stores the source in Supabase Storage.
Server sends the image and prompt to OpenAI Images for a 1536x1024 jpeg render.
Server stores the OpenAI render in Supabase Storage.
Server sends the OpenAI render to Topaz for a 4096x2048 jpeg upscale.
Client polls Topaz status; when completed, the server downloads and stores the final Topaz output, then returns a signed final panorama URL for the viewer.
securityBoundaries: Provider API keys and Supabase secret key are only read from server-only modules or API routes.
Browser/client auth uses only NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY.
Supabase SSR proxy uses getClaims to validate protected workspace access from cookies.
Supabase admin client uses disabled session persistence and token refresh.
Login, signup, and password recovery server actions check BETA_ALLOWED_EMAILS before calling Supabase Auth; with no configured emails, beta auth is closed.
Supabase schema enables row level security for projects, scenes, and render_jobs.
Render and Topaz API routes require Supabase Auth before provider or storage work.
Real beta rendering has a daily real render limit of 3 attempts per authenticated user.
Storage bucket is private by default and has owner-scoped storage.objects policies documented in the migration.

domainModel.authUsers: Supabase Auth manages user accounts; app-level beta gating only allows emails from BETA_ALLOWED_EMAILS and does not create a custom public users table.
plannedDatabaseEntities: render_jobs with owner_id, mode, started_at, provider process metadata, and failure messages

files.coverage: Unit and source-level checks for beta email allowlist parsing, 5-email cap, password confirmation validation, login/signup GSAP mode-switch wiring, beta error mapping, and env documentation.
Source and helper checks for real beta render mode, daily quota helpers, signed stored panorama URLs, provider failure handling, schema grants, and architecture documentation.

Source-level checks for public homepage content, GSAP ScrollTrigger homepage wiring, borderless dark glass styling hooks, pricing accent color regression, hero panorama preview asset, auth route structure, protected workspace routes, proxy guard, login/recovery actions, env documentation, and architecture docs.

Source-level checks for authenticated render/Topaz APIs, Supabase-backed workspace loading, create/upload/render UI wiring, password recovery, migration/env documentation, and provider request defaults.
Render status progress and mock state transitions.
Panorama file validation for supported types, dimensions, and 2:1 ratio.
Source-level regression checks that the current workspace does not expose removed scenes rail or jobs screen UI.

knownGaps: src/lib/mock-data.ts contains richer project and scene concepts that are not currently imported by WorkspaceApp.
The Supabase migration file has been added but still must be applied to the target project before persisted workspace data works outside environments where the schema already exists.
Google OAuth is intentionally out of v1 and no longer appears in the login UI.

maintenanceInstructions.purpose: Use this file as the fast orientation map before reading the whole repository.
whenToUpdate: Any route, screen, component, data model, provider integration, environment variable, storage/database schema, test responsibility, or major user flow changes.
Any file is added, removed, renamed, or changes responsibility in a way that affects app architecture.
Any known gap is fixed or a new gap appears.
howToUpdate: Keep this JSON valid and concise.
Prefer factual current-state notes over aspirational descriptions.
Update lastReviewed to the current date when making meaningful architecture changes.
Mention disconnected or partially wired pieces explicitly.
